EXH_0719.description_text_en: IM Heung-soon: An Encounter with an Unfinished Past MMCA Film & Video is screening selected films and single-channel videos by IM Heung-soon, whose exhibition Things that Do Us Part (30 Nov 2017 – 8 Apr 2018) was chosen for the MMCA Hyundai Motor Series 2017. This exhibition seeks to reflect on the contemporary history of Korea by dealing with the tragedy of the division of Korea and the joys and sorrows of the Korean working class. It is the culmination of the work that the artist has spent much of his life collecting and researching. Viewing each of his films and videos separately in a theater provokes responses that are quite unlike viewing them in an exhibition. His work embodies a dark side of Korean society—represented by its brutal history and the despair of its working class—and utilizes a delicate form created by the mixture of traditional documentary methods (including research and interviews) and emotionally ringing poetic reiteration. The tragic events of modern history, filled with conflict and antagonism, are recorded through the testimony of women who have quietly suffered for years. This invisible emotional power creates images, which form fragments of sorrow within the tragedies of our modern history. This program includes several feature films: Jeju Prayer (2012), a film dealing with the 4∙3 Uprising in Jeju and the construction of military bases in Gangjeong village; Factory Complex (2015), a look at the painful history of Korea's women workers; Reborn (2017), a chronicling of the stories of women who have suffered from the Korean War, the Vietnam War, and the Iran–Iraq War; and Ryeohaeng (2016), a depiction of the hopelessness and frustration of North Korean refugee women. Early short video essays such as The Way to Icheon (1998) and Basement My Love (2001) capture fragments of the artist's life as the child of a working-class family. Following these, his films become progressively more fable-like in their representation of the results of a tragic history. A scene from the short video, Next Life (2005), where an old woman wanders through a forest to encounter her dead husband, and the ravishing scenes of flying crows in Sung Si (Jeju Symptom and Sign) (2011) show reverberations of the 4∙3 Uprising in Jeju. Here, as always, IM Heung-soon's works are trying to provoke an encounter with an unfinished past. WED THU FRI SAT SUN 21 15:00 ⑫ⓀⒺ Short Program 1 (79min) 22 15:00 ⑫ⓀⒺ Ryeohaeng (86min) 23 15:00 ⑫ⓀⒺ Short Program 2 (78min) 19:00 ⑮ⓀⒺ Jeju Prayer (93min) 24 17:00 ⑮ⓀⒺ Things that Do Us Part (108min) Screening & Talk 25 15:00 ⑫ⓀⒺ Exchange Diary (61min) 28 15:00 ⑮ⓀⒺ Factory Complex (95min) 29 15:00 ⑫ⓀⒺ Short Program 3 (58min) ​ 30 15:00 ⑮ⓀⒺ Reborn (90min) 18:30 ⑫ⓀⒺ Exchange Diary (61min) Screening & Talk 31 15:00 ⑮ⓀⒺ Jeju Prayer (93min) Screening & Talk 4.1 15:00 ⑮ⓀⒺ Things that Do Us Part (108min) ​ ​ ※ A Forest of Symptoms Brochure ※ A Forest of Symptoms Screening Schedule

EXH_0917.description_text:
국립현대미술관 청주는 개방 수장고를 부분 개편하여 조각가 전뢰진(田礌鎭, 1929~)의 작품 17점을 한자리에 모아 선보인다. 전뢰진은 지난해 개최한 ‹팝업 수장고› 프로그램에 마련된 관람객 투표 코너에서 가장 보고 싶은 작품의 작가로 선정되었다. 투표 결과를 반영하여 국립현대미술관에서 소장하고 있는 전뢰진 조각 10점과 드로잉 7점을 개방 수장고 전면에 배치하고, 자료를 살펴볼 수 있는 전뢰진 공간을 조성하였다.

조각가 전뢰진은 평생 돌을 깎고 다듬어 작품을 만들어 왔다. 가족과 사랑, 낙원 등 인간애를 담고 있는 돌 조각은 보는 사람에게 따스함과 평화로움을 전달한다. 대학에서 석조각을 전공한 전뢰진은 국전(대한민국미술전람회), 대한미술협회전 등 많은 공모전에 출품하여 입상하였고, 특히 1957년 제6회 국전에서 문교부장관상을 수상하였다. 차갑고 단단한 돌에 생명력을 담으며 오직 한길을 걸어온 전뢰진의 조각 세계는 보는 사람에게 감동을 주기에 충분하다.

이번 개편에서는 1950년대부터 1990년대까지 전뢰진의 조각과 드로잉을 개방 수장고에 함께 설치하고, 작가 인터뷰 영상과 도록 자료를 배치하여 개방 수장고의 기능을 확장하였다. 관람객들은 작품과 여러 관련 자료를 통해 조각가 전뢰진의 작품 세계를 좀 더 깊이 있게 살펴볼 수 있을 것이다.
EXH_0917.description_text_en: The National Museum of Modern and Contemporary Art, Cheongju, is partially reorganizing its open storage and presenting 17 works by sculptor JEUN Loijin (born in 1929) in one space. JEUN Loijin was selected as the artist whose artworks the audience would most like to see through voting during the ‹ Pop-up Storage › program held last year. In reflection of the voting results, the National Museum of Modern and Contemporary Art has placed 10 sculptures and 7 drawings by the artist from its collections in the front space of the open storage and created a space for JEUN Loijin where visitors can enjoy other materials. Sculptor JEUN Loijin has carved and polished stones all his life to create his works. His stone sculptures that embody humanity, such as family, love, and paradise, convey a sense of warmth and peace to viewers. Having majored in Sculpture in college, he submitted his works to numerous open exhibitions and won prizes, including the National Art Exhibition of The Republic of Korea and the Exhibition of the Korea Art Association. In particular, he won the Minister of Culture and Education Award at the 6th National Art Exhibition of The Republic of Korea in 1957. JEUN Loijin has chosen the path of infusing life into cold and hard stones, and the world of his sculptures itself is enough to impress the audience. With the reorganization, JEUN Loijin's sculptures and drawings from the 1950s to the 1990s will be installed together in the open storage, and interview videos and catalog materials will be on display to expand the function of the storage. Visitors will be able to explore the world of sculptor JEUN Loijin more in-depth through his artworks and various relevant materials.
